app_info.subtitle: Review academic drafts
app_info.description: Academic Writing Toolkit helps users review pasted academic writing materials in ChatGPT. It can audit citation consistency between draft prose and reading-note source lines, check conservative British English spelling, flag paragraph-level logic issues, validate BibTeX records offline, and create a standard reading-notes Markdown template.

justifications.read_only_justification: Computes citation audit findings from user-provided text and writes only temporary files during the tool call.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Computes spelling findings and optional preview text from user-provided text without modifying user files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Computes paragraph-level review findings from user-provided text only.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Performs offline validation of user-provided BibTeX and does not contact external metadata APIs.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Generates a Markdown template from user-provided source metadata without storing it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

test_cases.description: Audit Harvard citation consistency between pasted chapter text and reading-note source lines.
test_cases.user_prompt: Check this draft citation against my reading note: chapter text 'Smith (2024) argues that archives shape interpretation.' reading note '**Source**: Jones, J. (2024) Title in sentence case. *Publisher*.'
test_cases.expected_output: Returns citation audit findings that identify the unmatched in-text citation and the unused source.

test_cases.description: Check British English spelling in pasted thesis prose.
test_cases.user_prompt: Please check this sentence for British English spelling: 'The color center helps analyze behavior.'
test_cases.expected_output: Returns conservative replacement suggestions such as color to colour, center to centre, analyze to analyse, and behavior to behaviour.

test_cases.description: Review paragraph logic for short paragraphs and repeated transitions.
test_cases.user_prompt: Review paragraph logic in this draft: 'However, this matters.\n\nHowever, it also matters.'
test_cases.expected_output: Returns paragraph logic findings for short paragraphs and repeated adjacent transition wording.

test_cases.user_prompt: Verify this BibTeX: @article{smith2024, title={Incomplete article}, year={2024}}
test_cases.expected_output: Returns offline BibTeX validation findings, including missing required article fields.

test_cases.description: Create a standard reading-note template for a source.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a Harvard reading note template for Smith, J., Test Article, 2024, relevant to Ch2 S2.1.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a Markdown reading notes template with Source, Date read, Status, Relevance, Key Arguments, Detailed Notes, Key Terms, Thesis Connections, and Questions sections.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for general essay writing without a request for toolkit checks.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Write a new essay about Renaissance literature.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because the user is asking ChatGPT to draft new prose rather than run an academic writing toolkit check.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for live web citation lookup.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Search the web for the newest articles about climate policy.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it does not perform web search or online metadata lookup.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for local file operations.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Open my local thesis folder and rewrite chapter 3.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it only processes text the user provides in ChatGPT and does not access local files.
